content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

September 15, 1976

A-06-292

Mr. Patrick J. Sampson
City Attorney
The City of Pomona
Box 660
Pomona, California 91769

Dear Mr. Sampson:

     Thank you for your letter of August 24, 1976, in which you
inquire about the status of Conflict of Interest Codes for joint
powers authorities. In answer to your first question, the Fair
Political Practices Commission has not set any deadlines for
Conflict of Interest Codes for joint powers authorities. The
Conflict of Interest Division of the Commission has surveyed
various joint powers authorities with jurisdiction in more than
one county to determine the general nature of their powers and
the possible resultant requirement that such authorities adopt
conflict of interest codes. We have not yet completed that study
and will not do so for some months. As a general proposition,
however, it is the view of the Conflict of Interest Division
that most joint powers authorities will be required to develop
Conflict of Interest Codes. In addition it seems apparent that
when members of various city councils sit on the governing boards
of such joint powers authorities, those persons will have disclosure
requirements pursuant to the Conflict of Interest Code for the
joint powers authority different from their reporting obligations
as city council persons under Govt. Code Section 87200. This is
because one's reporting requirements under both a Conflict of
Interest Code and under Government Code Section 87200 are tied to
interests which may be affected by the official within the juris-
diction of his or her authority. Since the geographical jurisdic-
tion of a city and of a joint powers authority may differ, the
reporting requirements for each may differ.

     One consolation to the requirement that board members of
joint powers authorities file statements of economic interest is
that the substantive jurisdiction of a joint powers authorities is
usually extremely narrow. Thus the reporting requirements will
be relatively narrow. For this reason and the fact that disclosure
is also tied to the geographical jurisdiction of an agency, the
"City of Mina" ordinance would not be sufficient to serve as the
code of a joint powers authority.


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6941

Finally it should be clear from the discussion above that it
would make no difference whether the joint powers authority board
was comprised solely of Council members who are filing Statements
of Economic Interest pursuant to Govt. Code Section 87200 or whether
the board was made up of city staff and/or private citizens.

     As general proposition these results seems somewhat harsh to
me personally. If you have any suggestion which would lead to a
less harsh result, please inform me and/or seek formal advice from
the Commission.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.,
                                        Chief, Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division
                                        916/322-6444

DLS:jhm

PATRICK J. SAMPSON

City Attorney

THE CITY OF POMONA

Box 660, Pomona, CA 91769 (714) 620-2071

Office of the City Attorney

August 24, 1976


STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Gentlemen:

I am the attorney for the Tri-City Mental Health Authority, as well as other
joint powers authorities which have been formed by Pomona and other cities in
the area.  Each board of the joint powers authorities, with one exception,
is made up with City Council members of the several cities.  One authority,
the Valley Association of Cities, does have board members or alternates who
are not City Council members.

Has the FPPC set a deadline for entity conflict of interest codes for joint
powers authorities?  Is the City of Nina ordinance sufficient for such code?
Does it make a difference whether the joint powers authority board is made up
solely of Council members who are themselves filing Proposition 9 statements
or if the board can be made up of city staff and/or private citizens?

Please answer these questions as expeditiously as possible.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
PATRICK J. SAMPSON
City Attorney

PJS:tb

sections.question: Has the FPPC set a deadline for entity conflict of interest codes for joint powers authorities? Is the City of Nina ordinance sufficient for such code? Does it make a difference whether the joint powers authority board is made up solely of Council members who are themselves filing Proposition 9 statements or if the board can be made up of city staff and/or private citizens?
sections.conclusion: The document indicates that it makes no material difference whether the joint powers authority board was comprised solely of Council members who are filing Statements of Interest pursuant to Govt. Code Section 87200 or whether the board was made up of city staff and/or private citizens. The FPPC notes that the results seem somewhat harsh as a general proposition and invites the requester to seek further formal advice if a less harsh result is desired.
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of Pomona seeks FPPC guidance on conflict of interest code requirements for joint powers authorities, including deadlines, whether an existing city ordinance is sufficient, and whether the composition of the board (Council members vs. staff/private citizens) affects the requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the composition of the joint powers authority board (whether solely Council members or including staff/private citizens) does not affect conflict of interest code requirements. The FPPC acknowledged the conclusion may seem harsh and invited the requester to seek formal advice if alternative interpretations were desired.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is fragmented and contains formatting issues typical of scanned documents from 1976. The conclusion section is present but somewhat unclear due to document quality. The question section is clearly identifiable from the incoming letter. The response is from Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief of the Conflicts of Interest Division.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Has the FPPC set a deadline for entity conflict of interest codes for joint powers authorities? Is the City of Nina ordinance sufficient for such code? Does it make a difference whether the joint powers authority board is made up solely of Council members who are themselves filing Proposition 9 statements or if the board can be made up of city staff and/or private citizens?

CONCLUSION: The document indicates that it makes no material difference whether the joint powers authority board was comprised solely of Council members who are filing Statements of Interest pursuant to Govt. Code Section 87200 or whether the board was made up of city staff and/or private citizens. The FPPC notes that the results seem somewhat harsh as a general proposition and invites the requester to seek further formal advice if a less harsh result is desired.

embedding.summary: An FPPC advice letter responding to questions from the City Attorney of Pomona regarding conflict of interest code requirements and deadlines for joint powers authorities. The letter addresses whether board composition affects the applicability of conflict of interest provisions.
